articles.title: Arsenal Win First Premier League Title in 22 Years
articles.summary: Arsenal were crowned Premier League champions on Tuesday 19 May 2026 after Manchester City drew 1-1 with Bournemouth, ending a 22-year wait that stretches back to the famous Invincibles season of 2003-04. Manager Mikel Arteta, who had finished runner-up three seasons in a row, finally got over the line with Arsenal sitting four points clear at the top with one game still to play. The Gunners now also have a Champions League final against Paris Saint-Germain on 30 May, giving them a shot at the greatest double in the club's history.
articles.content:
It is done. Arsenal are Premier League champions for the first time since 2004. The wait is over. Twenty-two years, four runners-up finishes, and more heartbreak than any fanbase should have to endure. On Tuesday evening, Manchester City drew 1-1 at Bournemouth, and that was enough. Arsenal did not even need to kick a ball.

The goal that set it in motion came from Bournemouth's Junior Kroupi in the 39th minute. Erling Haaland equalised for Manchester City deep into stoppage time, but the draw was all Arsenal needed. Manchester City are now four points behind with just one game left to play. The title belongs to north London.

For Mikel Arteta, this is personal. He took charge of Arsenal in 2019 and has watched the title slip away three seasons in a row. In 2023 and 2024, Manchester City chased down Arsenal's lead and overtook them. Last season, Liverpool pipped them. Three times a bridesmaid. Not this time. Arteta also becomes the first former Premier League player to win the title as a manager.

The numbers behind this Arsenal team are stunning. They have conceded just 26 goals across 37 league games, the fewest in the division, with goalkeeper David Raya winning the Coca-Cola Premier League Golden Glove for a third straight season. Arsenal also scored a league-high 24 goals from set pieces, with 18 of those coming from corners alone, the most by any side in the Premier League era. Viktor Gyokeres, signed last summer, led the scoring, supported by Bukayo Saka, Eberechi Eze, and Declan Rice among others.

Arsenal's last title came during the iconic Invincibles season of 2003-04, when Arsène Wenger's side went the entire league campaign unbeaten. That team had Thierry Henry, Patrick Vieira, Dennis Bergkamp. This Arsenal is writing its own legend. And there is still one more shot at glory. On 30 May, Arsenal face Paris Saint-Germain in the Champions League final. Win that, and this becomes the greatest season in the club's 140-year history.
keyPoints: Arsenal are 2025-26 Premier League champions, their first title in 22 years since the 2003-04 Invincibles season
The title was confirmed when Manchester City drew 1-1 with Bournemouth on 19 May, leaving Arsenal four points clear
Manager Mikel Arteta had finished runner-up three seasons in a row before finally winning the league
David Raya won the Coca-Cola Premier League Golden Glove for the third consecutive season with 19 clean sheets
Arsenal face Paris Saint-Germain in the Champions League final on 30 May, chasing an historic double

articles.title: Trump Bought Palantir and Dell Stocks, Then Publicly Praised Them
articles.summary: New federal ethics disclosures show Donald Trump executed thousands of stock trades in early 2026, and several of those stocks surged after he publicly praised the companies. Records filed with the U.S. Office of Government Ethics reveal more than 3,600 transactions between January and March 2026, totalling as much as $750 million. The pattern, buy the stock then applaud the company in public, is raising serious ethics questions even though no formal insider trading charges have been filed.
articles.content:
Donald Trump has filed new financial disclosures with the U.S. Office of Government Ethics, and the picture they paint is striking. Trump reported thousands of financial transactions totalling hundreds of millions of dollars, including large purchases and sales of tech giants Nvidia, Microsoft, Amazon and Meta, in the first three months of 2026. The cumulative value of the trades ranged from at least $220 million to as much as $750 million, since federal ethics disclosures only require broad valuation bands rather than precise figures.

The most eyebrow-raising example involves Palantir, the AI software company with major U.S. government contracts. Trump scooped up shares of Palantir weeks before he famously praised the stock on Truth Social. Weeks after buying, Trump praised Palantir on Truth Social, calling its military technology battle-tested and effective, writing: "Palantir Technologies (PLTR) has proven to have great war fighting capabilities and equipment. Just ask our enemies!!!" Then there is Dell Technologies. The filings show a major $1 million to $5 million entry into Dell Technologies Class C stock on February 10, 2026, which preceded Trump's public endorsement of Dell hardware during a White House event. Dell Technologies shares surged as much as 14.6% intraday on May 8, reaching an all-time high of $263.99.

The Thermo Fisher story is perhaps the most blunt example of all. On March 11, Trump took a tour of Ohio-based medical tech company Thermo Fisher, said "It's a great company" in front of cameras alongside CEO Marc Casper, and as we now know, in the weeks leading up to that seemingly random visit, Trump's trust purchased as much as $215,000 worth of stock options in Thermo Fisher. The pattern repeats across Nvidia too. Trump purchased $1 million to $5 million worth of Nvidia stock on February 10, only a week before Nvidia announced a major computer processing power deal with Meta.

U.S. presidents are not banned from trading financial markets but must disclose personal trades. No charges have been filed and no proven acts of insider trading have been outlined, but the revelation draws ethics scrutiny and a push for trading restrictions. Trump Organization representatives told CNBC that the president's investments are managed through discretionary accounts overseen by independent financial institutions, noting that neither Trump nor his family directly controls specific trades. Still, assuming holdings remained relatively the same since the end of March, Trump is 20% or more in profit on almost all the names indicated. The calls to close the loophole are growing louder, but for now, no comprehensive ban has yet become law.
keyPoints: Trump filed disclosures revealing 3,600+ stock trades worth up to $750 million in Q1 2026
Trump bought Palantir stock then publicly praised it on Truth Social weeks later
Trump bought Dell stock in February; Dell hit an all-time high after his White House endorsement in May
Trump bought Nvidia stock a week before Nvidia announced a major deal with Meta
No insider trading charges have been filed; the White House says trades are managed independently
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Donald Trump financial disclosure stock trading 2026

articles.title: Ronaldo, 41, Makes History at a Record Sixth World Cup
articles.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo has been named in Portugal's squad for the 2026 World Cup, making him the first player ever to appear at six FIFA World Cup tournaments. At 41 years old, Ronaldo will lead Portugal into Group K against DR Congo, Uzbekistan, and Colombia. Coach Roberto Martinez made the squad announcement deeply personal, declaring that the late Diogo Jota will travel with the team as the spiritual 27th player.
articles.content:
Cristiano Ronaldo is officially going to a sixth World Cup. Portugal coach Roberto Martinez announced the squad on Tuesday, and Ronaldo's name was right there at the top. No other player in the history of the tournament has ever done this. Six. World Cups. At 41 years old.

Martinez named a 27-player group for the tournament, with Portugal drawn into Group K to face DR Congo, Uzbekistan, and Colombia. But the announcement carried a weight far beyond tactics and selections. Martinez dedicated the entire campaign to Diogo Jota, the Liverpool forward who died in a car crash in northern Spain on 3 July 2025, alongside his brother André Silva. Jota was just 28. He had won 49 caps for Portugal and scored 14 international goals, and his final game for the national team was a Nations League final victory over Spain.

Martinez said the squad would travel to the World Cup with 27 players "plus one." He described Jota as "our strength, our joy" and said that "Diogo Jota's spirit, strength and example are the plus one and will always be the plus one." It was a moment that reminded everyone this tournament means more than football for Portugal right now.

For Ronaldo, this is the closing chapter of the greatest individual career the sport has ever seen. He has played at every World Cup since 2006. Whether Portugal can go all the way this summer remains to be seen, but one thing is already certain: nobody will ever do what Ronaldo is doing right now.
keyPoints: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes the first player in history to appear at six FIFA World Cups
Ronaldo is 41 years old and will lead Portugal in Group K against DR Congo, Uzbekistan, and Colombia
Coach Roberto Martinez named 27 players plus a symbolic tribute to the late Diogo Jota as the squad's spiritual extra member
Diogo Jota died in a car crash in Spain in July 2025 at age 28, alongside his brother André Silva
Jota scored 14 goals in 49 caps for Portugal and his final match was a Nations League final win over Spain

articles.title: Google Gemini Gets Spark, Daily Brief and Omni to Fight ChatGPT
articles.summary: Google unveiled a wave of major updates to its Gemini app at I/O 2026, introducing a new AI agent called Gemini Spark, a morning digest feature called Daily Brief, a redesigned interface called Neural Expressive, and a new video model called Gemini Omni. The moves signal Google's push to transform Gemini from a reactive chatbot into a full-time personal AI hub to compete with ChatGPT and Claude. Gemini already reaches more than 900 million monthly users across 230 countries.
articles.content:
Google just made its biggest move yet in the AI assistant wars. At Google I/O 2026 on Tuesday, the company announced a sweeping set of updates to the Gemini app designed to take on ChatGPT and Claude head-on.

The headliner is Gemini Spark, a cloud-based AI agent built on the new Gemini 3.5 Flash model. Google describes Gemini Spark as a 24/7 personal AI agent that does real work on your behalf, handling tasks across Gmail, Docs, and other Google services even when your phone is locked or your laptop is closed. Because Gemini Spark runs entirely in the cloud, your device never needs to stay active for it to keep working. Gemini Spark is currently in testing and will roll out to Google AI Ultra subscribers next week.

Alongside Gemini Spark, Google introduced Daily Brief, a personalized morning digest that pulls from your inbox, calendar, and task list to give you a prioritized overview of your day. It does not just summarize things either. Daily Brief suggests next steps and puts the most urgent items at the top. Daily Brief is rolling out today to Google AI subscribers in the United States. The app also got a visual overhaul called Neural Expressive, which replaces walls of text with bold headlines, inline images, timelines, and interactive visuals as you scroll.

On the video side, Google launched Gemini Omni, a new multimodal model that can take any combination of text, images, audio, and video as input and generate polished video output grounded in Gemini's real-world knowledge. Gemini Omni is rolling out to Google Flow and YouTube Shorts. To address obvious concerns about deepfakes and manipulated footage, Google says each video generated with Gemini Omni will carry a SynthID digital watermark.

Google also made its premium tier more competitive. The Google AI Ultra subscription dropped from $250 per month to $100, giving subscribers five times the usage limits of the $20 AI Pro plan, 20 terabytes of cloud storage, YouTube Premium, and beta access to Gemini Spark. With 900 million monthly users already on board, Google is clearly betting that Gemini is not just another chatbot. It wants Gemini to be the AI layer running quietly across every corner of your digital life.
keyPoints: Gemini Spark is a new cloud-based AI agent that keeps working in the background even when your phone is locked
Daily Brief is a new personalized morning digest pulling from your inbox, calendar, and task list
Neural Expressive is a new design language replacing text walls with visual, scrollable responses
Gemini Omni is a new multimodal video model rolling out to Google Flow and YouTube Shorts
Google AI Ultra dropped from $250 to $100 per month to sharpen competition with ChatGPT and Claude

articles.title: Southampton Expelled from Championship Playoff Final for Secretly Filming Rival Training Sessions
articles.summary: Southampton have been thrown out of the EFL Championship playoff final after admitting they secretly filmed the training sessions of three opponents during the 2025-26 season. An independent disciplinary commission ruled on May 19 that Middlesbrough, who Southampton beat in the semi-finals, are reinstated and will now face Hull City at Wembley on Saturday May 23. Southampton also received a four-point deduction for next season.
articles.content:
This is one of the wildest scandals English football has seen in years. Southampton have been kicked out of Saturday's Championship playoff final and docked four points for the 2026-27 season after admitting to spying on three different opponents during the course of the season. The EFL Championship playoff final is essentially the most valuable single game in world football. The playoff final is labeled the world's richest one-off soccer match because a windfall of at least £200 million in future earnings is on offer for the winning team.

The scandal, now widely dubbed "Spygate", started when Middlesbrough caught a Southampton staff member at their training ground. Southampton were initially reported to the EFL by Middlesbrough when they alleged a Southampton staff member had spied on a training session on May 7 ahead of their play-off semi-final, in breach of league rules, with the EFL charging them the following day. Pictures subsequently emerged the following week that showed an analyst with his phone, seemingly recording Middlesbrough's session, immediately casting into doubt Southampton's 2-1 aggregate win.

Things snowballed fast. Further charges were levelled at Southampton on May 17 in relation to additional breaches during the 2025-26 season, corresponding to their fixture against Oxford United in December 2025 and Ipswich Town in April 2026. Southampton admitted breaches of regulations requiring clubs to act with the utmost good faith and prohibiting the observation of another club's training session within 72 hours of a scheduled match. Awkwardly, of the three games that Southampton admitted to spying before, they won none, losing at Oxford and drawing against Ipswich Town and Middlesbrough.

Middlesbrough have been reinstated after the bombshell verdict from the independent commission and will now face Hull City in the play-off final on Saturday May 23. In addition, Southampton have been handed a four-point deduction for next season, and they have submitted an appeal which will be heard on Wednesday, with the EFL acknowledging this could result in a further change to Saturday's fixture. Southampton were relegated from the Premier League last season and were desperately chasing an immediate return. Now, instead of a shot at Wembley, they face a points penalty and a cloud of disgrace hanging over their entire campaign.
keyPoints: Southampton admitted to secretly filming training sessions of Oxford United, Ipswich Town and Middlesbrough
An independent EFL commission expelled Southampton from the Championship playoff final on May 19
Middlesbrough are reinstated and will face Hull City at Wembley on Saturday May 23
Southampton also received a four-point deduction for the 2026-27 season
Southampton have appealed the decision; a final ruling is due today, May 20

articles.title: Three Commonwealth Short Story Prize Winners Suspected of Using AI Chatbots
articles.summary: Three of the five regional winners of the 2026 Commonwealth Short Story Prize are suspected of having used AI chatbots to write their entries, in a scandal that is shaking the literary world. The prestigious prize, which whittled 7,806 entries down to five winners, has been rocked by accusations that at least one story was rated 100 percent AI-generated by detection tool Pangram. The Commonwealth Foundation says it is conducting a "thorough, transparent review" but admits it does not currently use AI checkers in its judging process.
articles.content:
The Commonwealth Short Story Prize is one of literature's most competitive awards. This year, 7,806 writers from across the countries of the British Commonwealth submitted unpublished short fiction. A team of readers cut that down to 200, then to 25, then to five regional winners. The odds of making it through were tougher than getting into Harvard. And now three of those five winners are suspected of having used an AI chatbot to write their stories.

The scandal burst into public view when Wharton professor Ethan Mollick, who studies AI's effects on education and the workplace, ran one of the winning stories through Pangram, an AI detection program. Pangram rated "The Serpent in the Grove" by Jamir Nazir, the regional winner from the Caribbean, as 100 percent AI-generated. Nazir's story follows a rum-drinking farmer who comes across an enchanted grove, and a panel of judges chaired by celebrated English novelist Louise Doughty had praised its "precise yet richly evocative" language. The story was published by Granta, the iconic literary magazine, as part of its partnership with the prize.

The Commonwealth Foundation, which runs the prize, has responded carefully. In a public statement, the Foundation said it is aware of the allegations and is committed to responding "with care and transparency." It explained that it does not currently use AI checkers in its judging process because submitting unpublished fiction to a third-party AI tool raises serious concerns around consent and artistic ownership. Granta, for its part, said its editors "were not involved with these stories or their selection beyond copy-editing them upon receipt."

The broader problem here is that reliable AI detection is still an unsolved puzzle. Critics of AI detectors point out that these tools are not always accurate, even the well-respected ones like Pangram. The Foundation acknowledged as much, stating that until "a sufficient tool or process to reliably detect the use of AI emerges," the prize must "operate on the principle of trust." That is a difficult position to defend when three of your five winners are under suspicion.

What makes this story bigger than one prize is what it signals about the creative world right now. Literary contests, academic submissions, journalism and screenwriting are all facing the same crisis. The technology to generate convincing prose is widely available and free to use. The technology to reliably detect it is not. The 2026 Commonwealth Short Story Prize may be the highest-profile example yet of that gap, but it is almost certainly not the last. As Wired put it, this is quickly starting to feel like the new normal.
keyPoints: Three of five 2026 Commonwealth Short Story Prize regional winners are suspected of using AI chatbots
Jamir Nazir's winning story "The Serpent in the Grove" was rated 100 percent AI-generated by detection tool Pangram
The Commonwealth Foundation does not use AI checkers in its judging process due to concerns about unpublished fiction and consent
The Foundation says it is conducting a thorough and transparent review of its selection process
The case highlights the wider crisis facing all creative industries as AI writing tools outpace detection technology

articles.title: Warhorse Studios Confirms Lord of the Rings Open-World RPG
articles.summary: Warhorse Studios, the Czech developer behind Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2, has officially confirmed it is building an open-world RPG set in Middle-earth, the world of The Lord of the Rings. The studio posted the announcement on X today, May 20, also confirming a new Kingdom Come adventure is in development alongside it. No release windows or platforms have been announced yet.
articles.content:
Warhorse Studios is the Prague-based team that made Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2, a gritty, historically grounded RPG set in medieval Bohemia that launched earlier this year to a 95 percent recommended rating on OpenCritic and sold so well it broke even on its budget on day one. Now the studio is heading somewhere very different indeed.

Posting on X today, Warhorse Studios ended the speculation with a simple, direct announcement. The studio is working on an open-world Middle-earth RPG set in the universe of The Lord of the Rings. In the same post, it confirmed a new Kingdom Come adventure is also in the pipeline. The message ended with the studio saying it is excited to share more details when the time is right.

The Lord of the Rings rumours had been circulating for months. A Polish translator who previously worked with CD Projekt Red had suggested a Tolkien game was in development at Warhorse. The studio kept hinting at something big, describing its next project as a huge, immersive RPG without confirming what it was. Today that tease became official.

The licence connection makes sense when you follow the corporate trail. Warhorse Studios is owned by Plaion, which sits under the Embracer Group. Embracer acquired Middle-earth Enterprises and the global rights to The Lord of the Rings IP back in 2022, and Embracer's COO has previously talked publicly about wanting to see a renaissance of Tolkien games. Handing the open-world RPG to Warhorse, arguably their most accomplished RPG studio, is the logical move.

For fans this is genuinely exciting news. The last great Lord of the Rings games came out around the time of the Peter Jackson film trilogy. Warhorse's reputation for layered storytelling, weight-driven melee combat, and worlds full of lived-in detail suggests a Middle-earth experience unlike anything that has come before. Both games are likely in early development, so the wait will be real. But the journey to Middle-earth has officially begun.
keyPoints: Warhorse Studios has officially confirmed it is making an open-world Lord of the Rings RPG set in Middle-earth
A new Kingdom Come adventure is also confirmed and in development at the same time
The announcement was made on X on May 20, 2026, with no platforms or release dates given yet
Warhorse is owned by Plaion under Embracer Group, which acquired the Lord of the Rings IP rights in 2022
Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 earned a 95 percent recommended rating on OpenCritic earlier this year, making Warhorse one of the most trusted RPG studios working today
articles.wikiSearchQuery: The Lord of the Rings video game

articles.title: Google and Samsung Unveil Gemini Smart Glasses with Warby Parker and Gentle Monster
articles.summary: Google and Samsung revealed the real-world designs of their upcoming smart glasses at Google I/O 2026, built in partnership with eyewear brands Warby Parker and Gentle Monster. The glasses run Google Gemini AI via voice commands and are set to launch this fall. This is the first concrete look at the product, and it puts Google and Samsung directly in Meta's Ray-Ban territory.
articles.content:
Samsung Electronics and Google unveiled new intelligent eyewear at Google I/O 2026, giving a first look at two premium styles created with eyewear partners Gentle Monster and Warby Parker. This is a big deal because, up until now, the project had been more of a promise than a product. This is the first time the product feels real rather than theoretical, with leaked renders last month showing a low-profile design that reads as regular glasses, not a tech accessory.

The products are entering a category that has so far been led by Meta Platforms, and are the first in a series of AI-powered glasses that will let consumers interact with Google Gemini through voice commands, similar to the way Meta AI runs on Ray-Ban smart glasses. The two styles cover opposite ends of the fashion spectrum. The Gentle Monster model emphasizes a unique, bold style while the Warby Parker model features a traditional, classic design, and both are engineered as lightweight frames for comfortable, all-day wear.

So what can they actually do? The devices do not feature a display. Instead they are equipped with built-in speakers, microphones and cameras, allowing the glasses to perceive the user's surroundings in real time and execute various functions through voice commands alone. Through Gemini voice commands, you can ask for navigation, get local suggestions like a nearby coffee shop, or place a pickup order. The glasses summarize text notifications and add calendar events hands-free. Real-time translation works two ways: audio matched to the speaker's voice, or text from menus and signs in your line of sight.

Models with built-in displays are in the works and set for release in 2027, but the Warby Parker and Gentle Monster hardware will predominantly be based on audio, with integrated cameras for Gemini. Pricing and a firm launch date are still coming, with Samsung and Google promising more details in the months ahead. The race to own your face is officially on.
keyPoints: Google and Samsung revealed smart glasses designs at Google I/O 2026 on May 19
Two styles: a bold Gentle Monster model and a classic Warby Parker model
Glasses run Google Gemini AI via voice, with cameras, microphones and speakers but no display
Features include navigation, real-time translation, notifications and hands-free calendar control
Fall 2026 launch planned; display-equipped models coming in 2027
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Samsung Google smart glasses Android XR

articles.title: Taiwan Travelogue Wins Booker — First Novel Originally in Mandarin
articles.summary: Yáng Shuāng-zǐ's Taiwan Travelogue, translated into English by Lin King, won the 2026 International Booker Prize at a ceremony at London's Tate Modern, becoming the first work translated from Mandarin Chinese to win the award in its ten-year history. Yáng and Lin King are also the first Taiwanese and Taiwanese-American winners of the prize, and they share a £50,000 award equally. The novel is a queer historical romance set in 1930s Japanese-occupied Taiwan.
articles.content:
History was made in London on Tuesday night. Taiwanese author Yáng Shuāng-zǐ and Taiwanese-American translator Lin King won the 2026 International Booker Prize for Taiwan Travelogue at a ceremony in the Turbine Hall at Tate Modern, taking home £50,000 split equally between them.

The International Booker Prize is awarded each year to the best work of fiction translated into English. Taiwan Travelogue is the first book translated from Mandarin Chinese to win the award in its ten-year history, and Yáng and Lin King are the first Taiwanese and Taiwanese-American winners. Judge chair Natasha Brown summed it up simply: "Can love overcome a power imbalance?" Brown said Taiwan Travelogue pulls off the incredible double feat of succeeding as both a romance and an incisive postcolonial novel.

The story follows Aoyama Chizuko, a Japanese writer with what the Booker Prize Foundation calls a monstrous appetite, on a government-sponsored tour of Japanese-occupied Taiwan in 1938. There she develops a complex and charged relationship with her Taiwanese interpreter, Chizuru. What makes the novel even more delightfully strange is its structure. Yáng published the original Mandarin Chinese version in 2020 listing herself as the translator of a fictional rediscovered Japanese memoir, not the author. When it first came out in Taiwan, some readers genuinely believed it was real. The book carries footnotes from both fictional and real translators, multiple afterwords, and dialogue woven across Japanese, Mandarin, and Taiwanese Hokkien.

The novel already had serious awards pedigree before Tuesday night. Lin King's English translation won the 2024 US National Book Award for Translated Literature, the first for Taiwanese literature. Yáng had earlier won Taiwan's highest literary honour, the Golden Tripod Award, for the original Mandarin Chinese version. The book was also the bookmakers' favourite heading into Tuesday's ceremony, with rights sold in 23 territories.

Translator Lin King put it best when describing her approach to the material. She said she personally dislikes historical fiction that is strictly miserable, because no matter how difficult the times, humans always find levity and love. Lin King's point is that reducing Taiwan's history under Japanese colonial rule purely to suffering erases the full humanity of its people. Taiwan Travelogue refuses to do that, and the Booker judges clearly agreed.
keyPoints: Taiwan Travelogue by Yáng Shuāng-zǐ is the first Mandarin Chinese novel to win the International Booker Prize
Author Yáng Shuāng-zǐ and translator Lin King are the first Taiwanese and Taiwanese-American Booker winners
The £50,000 prize is split equally between author and translator
Lin King's translation had already won the 2024 US National Book Award for Translated Literature
Yáng originally published the book as a fictional translation, listing herself as translator rather than author — and some readers believed it was real

articles.title: Alcaraz Out of Wimbledon 2026 With Right Wrist Injury
articles.summary: Carlos Alcaraz has confirmed he will not defend his title at Wimbledon this summer after a right wrist injury picked up at the Barcelona Open in April has refused to heal in time. The withdrawal means the world No. 2 will miss back-to-back Grand Slams for the first time since 2020, having already pulled out of the French Open. Alcaraz posted on social media: "My recovery is going well and I'm feeling much better, but unfortunately I'm still not ready to compete."
articles.content:
Carlos Alcaraz is out of Wimbledon 2026. The two-time champion confirmed on Tuesday, 19 May that a lingering right wrist injury means he will not set foot on the grass at the All England Club this summer. It is a huge blow for the sport and for the tournament.

Alcaraz injured his right wrist at the Barcelona Open on 14 April and has not played a single match since. The injury already forced him to withdraw from the Madrid Open, the Italian Open and the French Open, which begins this weekend. Wimbledon is now the fourth tournament he has had to skip. That makes this the first time since 2020, a season wrecked by the COVID-19 pandemic, that Alcaraz has missed consecutive Grand Slams.

To understand why this hurts so much, you need a little backstory. Alcaraz won back-to-back Wimbledon titles in 2023 and 2024, beating Novak Djokovic in both finals. He came agonisingly close to a third straight title in 2025 but lost to Jannik Sinner in the final. Then earlier this year, Alcaraz won the 2026 Australian Open to become the youngest man ever to win all four major titles in tennis. He was riding the peak of his powers before this injury hit.

With Alcaraz gone, world No. 1 Jannik Sinner becomes the massive favourite at both Roland Garros and Wimbledon. Sinner has already been on a tear, claiming six straight Masters 1000 trophies while Alcaraz has been sidelined. If Sinner wins both majors, he will close the Grand Slam title gap to just one. Alcaraz, for his part, says he is focused on getting back for the US hard-court swing and defending his US Open title in August. Fans will be hoping he makes it.
keyPoints: Carlos Alcaraz withdraws from Wimbledon 2026 due to a right wrist injury sustained at the Barcelona Open in April
He has already missed the French Open, Madrid Open and Italian Open with the same injury
This is his first back-to-back Grand Slam absence since the 2020 COVID-disrupted season
Alcaraz won Wimbledon in 2023 and 2024 and was beaten by Sinner in the 2025 final
Jannik Sinner is now the overwhelming favourite to win both the French Open and Wimbledon

articles.title: Jung Kook's CKJK Calvin Klein Collab Is Inspired by Motorcycles
articles.summary: Jung Kook of BTS has dropped his first-ever fashion collaboration, CKJK, a co-designed capsule with Calvin Klein that went live on May 19. The collection pulls directly from Jung Kook's love of motorcycles, a hobby his own fanbase has mixed feelings about. Prices run from $29 to $699 and the full collection is available now on calvinklein.com.
articles.content:
Jung Kook, the BTS superstar who became a Calvin Klein global brand ambassador back in 2023, has finally turned that partnership into something you can actually wear. The CKJK capsule collection dropped online on May 19 at 6 p.m. ET, and it hit select Calvin Klein stores and wholesale partners on May 20.

The collection is built around a hobby that not everyone in Jung Kook's fanbase is thrilled about: his passion for motorcycles. Jung Kook was licensed to ride a motorcycle in South Korea back in 2022, and his love for the pastime has been heavily documented on his Instagram and on BTS' Disney+ show Are You Sure?! That biker energy runs through every single piece.

Jung Kook's love of roughing it on the road is referenced in every piece of the capsule, from Americana ringer tees and cozy hoodies to grungy leather jackets. The collection's most distinctive design element is the integration of Jung Kook's actual tattoos, printed directly onto select shirts and bralettes throughout the capsule. Retail prices range from $29 to $699.

Jung Kook was clear that this was not a passive endorsement deal. "Partnering with Calvin Klein over the past few years has given me the opportunity to express myself through the brand's iconic style," he said in a statement, adding: "As my first fashion collaboration, I wanted to fully immerse myself in the process, leveraging Calvin Klein's design expertise to leave my mark on every piece."

Limited edition men's and women's underwear styles are available exclusively at Calvin Klein's Harajuku, Soho, and Champs-Élysées flagship stores. Retail pop-ups are planned across Los Angeles, Singapore, Thailand, Sydney, Melbourne, Taipei, Kuala Lumpur, Shenzhen, Chongqing, and Shanghai, which tells you everything about just how global Jung Kook's reach really is. If you want in, head to calvinklein.com/CKJK now before it sells out.
keyPoints: Jung Kook co-designed the CKJK capsule with Calvin Klein, his first ever fashion collaboration
The entire collection is inspired by his love of riding motorcycles
Jung Kook's real tattoos are printed onto select pieces in the collection
Prices range from $29 to $699 and it is live now at calvinklein.com
Pop-ups are running across cities worldwide including Los Angeles, Singapore, and Sydney

articles.title: Jackson Pollock's Number 7A Sells for Record $181M at Christie's
articles.summary: Jackson Pollock's Number 7A, 1948 sold for $181.2 million at Christie's in New York on Monday 18 May, nearly tripling Pollock's previous auction record of $61.2 million. The sale makes Number 7A, 1948 the fourth most expensive artwork ever sold at auction. The deal was done in just seven minutes of bidding.
articles.content:
Art history was made on Monday night at Christie's in New York. Jackson Pollock's Number 7A, 1948 sold for $181.2 million, including fees, smashing the previous auction record for the abstract expressionist artist by nearly three times. That previous record was $61.2 million, set in 2021 when Pollock's Number 17 (1951) sold at Sotheby's. Nobody saw the gap close this fast.

The painting is a massive work, more than three metres wide, featuring black drips of paint punctuated by flecks of red. Christie's described it as the moment where Pollock "frees himself from the shackles of conventional easel painting and produces one of the first truly abstract paintings in the history of art." Abstract expressionism, for the uninitiated, was a postwar American art movement built on spontaneous, emotional, large-scale painting rather than representing real objects. Pollock, who died in 1956, was its most famous practitioner, best known for his signature drip technique, where he laid canvases flat on the floor and poured or flung paint across them.

Number 7A, 1948 came from the private collection of the late Condé Nast chairman S.I. Newhouse, and the bidding for it started at $82 million before escalating into a three-way contest that lasted seven minutes. The hammer fell at $157 million, with fees pushing the final price to $181.2 million. The sale made Number 7A, 1948 the fourth most expensive artwork ever sold at auction, according to ARTnews. For context, the most expensive painting ever sold at auction remains Salvator Mundi, attributed to Leonardo da Vinci, which fetched $450 million in 2017.

The Pollock sale was the centrepiece of a record-breaking evening. Christie's combined two auctions, the Masterpieces: The Private Collection of S.I. Newhouse sale and the 20th Century Evening Sale, which together totalled more than $1.1 billion. That is only the second time in history that a single auction night has crossed the billion-dollar mark, with 97% of lots sold. Romanian sculptor Constantin Brancusi's bronze Danaïde also set a record, selling for $107.6 million, the second-highest price ever paid at auction for a sculpture.
keyPoints: Jackson Pollock's Number 7A, 1948 sold for a record $181.2 million at Christie's New York
The sale nearly tripled Pollock's previous auction record of $61.2 million set in 2021
Number 7A, 1948 is now the fourth most expensive artwork ever sold at auction
Bidding lasted just seven minutes in a three-way contest starting at $82 million
The Christie's evening total exceeded $1.1 billion, only the second time in auction history

articles.title: Spotify Adds Verified by Spotify Badges to Fight AI Podcast Fakes
articles.summary: Spotify launched its Verified by Spotify badge for podcasts on May 19, rolling out a green checkmark on show pages and in search results to help listeners identify real creators among a growing wave of AI-generated imposters. The move also comes with a toughened impersonation policy: Spotify will now remove any podcast that clones a host's voice or likeness without permission. The rollout starts with select shows today, with a wider expansion planned over the coming months.
articles.content:
If you have ever stumbled across a podcast that sounded a little off and wondered whether you were actually listening to a real person, Spotify has heard you. On May 19, Spotify officially launched Verified by Spotify for podcasts. The badge features a light green checkmark and will appear on show pages and in search results, giving listeners a visible signal that a podcast is the genuine article.

This is not Spotify's first move against AI slop. About three weeks earlier, on April 30, Spotify rolled out Verified by Spotify for music artists, where it promised that more than 99 percent of artists listeners actively search for would be verified. The podcast launch is the natural next step. Spotify currently hosts around 7 million podcast titles, and the concern is real: AI tools have made it easier than ever to clone a host's voice, slap a familiar-sounding name on a show, and pass it off as the real thing.

To qualify for the badge, a podcast needs to clear three bars. Spotify looks for sustained listener activity and consistent audience engagement over time, good standing with Spotify's platform rules, and verified audience authenticity, meaning the listenership is real humans rather than a bot farm inflating the play count. The rollout starts with select shows and will expand over the coming months. Alongside the badge, Spotify is formally banning podcasts that use AI voice cloning or any other method to impersonate a creator or host without their permission, and those shows will be removed.

It is worth noting what the badge does and does not tell you. Verified by Spotify confirms that a show is who it claims to be. It does not guarantee that the content inside is entirely human-made. A legitimately owned show could still use AI-generated scripts or synthetic narration and technically qualify for the checkmark. Spotify has not addressed that gap yet, which means the badge solves the identity problem without fully solving the content-authenticity problem.

Still, this matters. The rise of fake podcasts cloning real hosts is not a distant threat. It is already happening, and a visible trust signal in search results is a real improvement for listeners trying to find shows they can rely on. Spotify called this "the first in a series of steps" toward a more trustworthy podcast ecosystem. Whether that means deeper AI content disclosure requirements down the road remains to be seen, but the direction is clearly right.
keyPoints: Spotify launched Verified by Spotify badges for podcasts on May 19 with a green checkmark in search and on show pages
Spotify will now remove any podcast that impersonates a host via AI voice cloning or any other method
Badge eligibility requires sustained real listener engagement, policy compliance, and no bot-driven listenership
The rollout starts with select shows and expands over coming months across Spotify's 7 million podcast titles
The badge confirms a show's identity but does not indicate whether its content was made by a human or AI

articles.title: Rick and Morty Movie Confirmed at Warner Bros. With Jacob Hair Directing
articles.summary: Dan Harmon has confirmed a Rick and Morty movie is officially in development at Warner Bros., with franchise insider Jacob Hair in talks to direct. Hair has served as a supervising director on the Adult Swim show since 2019 and is Harmon's first and only choice for the job. Plot details are being kept tightly under wraps.
articles.content:
It is really happening. Dan Harmon, the co-creator of Rick and Morty, has officially confirmed that a feature film based on the Adult Swim series is in early development at Warner Bros., with Jacob Hair in talks to direct. Harmon had been teasing the idea for years, and now it is real.

Rick and Morty is an Emmy-winning adult animated series following genius, alcoholic mad scientist Rick Sanchez and his anxious grandson Morty Smith as they split time between suburban family life and dimension-hopping adventures across an infinite multiverse. The show has been on the air since 2013 and has built one of the most passionate fanbases in animation history.

A quick note on the show's turbulent recent history: Justin Roiland, who originally voiced both Rick and Morty, exited the series in 2023 amid felony domestic abuse charges that were ultimately dismissed, with Ian Cardoni and Harry Belden now voicing Rick and Morty respectively. The show kept going and, by most accounts, did not miss a beat.

Harmon was not shy about why Hair was the only name on the director shortlist. Harmon said he wanted Hair to direct the movie since talks about the project first began, describing him as "the director version of Donald Glover in that I don't know if there's a limitation to what he can do; we haven't found it yet." Hair has been with the show since 2019, directing numerous episodes and serving as supervising director.

Harmon has alluded to plans for "a super badass" feature-length episode for a number of years. His philosophy, as he put it back in 2023, was to "just take a Rick and Morty adventure, and spend a bunch of extra money on it and make it 90 minutes long." So do not expect the movie to suddenly turn into a heavy dramatic saga. Expect the same chaotic, interdimensional madness you love, just bigger.

The film will be animated just like the TV show rather than go a live-action route, though it is still unclear if it will get a theatrical release like The Simpsons Movie and The Bob's Burgers Movie did, or if it will be released as an Adult Swim or HBO Max exclusive. Rick and Morty is set to launch its ninth season this Sunday and has been renewed through season 12, so there is plenty of Rick and Morty universe to keep fans busy while the movie comes together.
keyPoints: Dan Harmon has officially confirmed a Rick and Morty movie is in development at Warner Bros.
Jacob Hair, supervising director of the show since 2019, is in talks to direct
Harmon described Hair as his first and only choice, comparing him to Donald Glover
The movie will be animated, not live action, but theatrical vs. streaming release is still undecided
Rick and Morty season 9 premieres on Adult Swim on May 24, 2026

articles.title: Nashville Lands Super Bowl LXIV in 2030 at New Nissan Stadium
articles.summary: NFL owners voted unanimously to award Super Bowl LXIV to Nashville, making it the first time the Music City will host the game. The announcement came Tuesday at the NFL Spring League Meeting in Orlando, with all 32 teams approving the bid. The game will be played at the new $2.1 billion Nissan Stadium, which is set to open in 2027.
articles.content:
Nashville is getting a Super Bowl. On Tuesday, NFL owners voted 32 to 0 to award Super Bowl LXIV to the Tennessee Titans' new home in 2030, making Nashville the latest American city to join the biggest sporting event on the planet.

The game will be played at the brand new Nissan Stadium, a $2.1 billion venue that is scheduled to open in 2027. That gives the Titans three full seasons to iron out any kinks before the Super Bowl spotlight lands on their doorstep. Tennessee Titans controlling owner Amy Adams Strunk called it an exciting moment for the entire state, saying the world will get to see the energy and culture that make Nashville so special.

NFL Commissioner Roger Goodell pointed to Nashville's track record with big events as part of the reason for the decision. When Nashville hosted the 2019 NFL Draft, it drew 600,000 fans, which Goodell called one of the greatest fan events in league history. That momentum, combined with the new stadium, made the case hard to ignore.

For those tracking the Super Bowl calendar, Super Bowl LXI goes to SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California on February 14, 2027. Super Bowl LXII follows at Atlanta's Mercedes-Benz Stadium in 2028, and Las Vegas hosts Super Bowl LXIII in 2029. Nashville closes out that run in 2030. Now the real question everyone in Music City is asking: will country music finally get its moment on the halftime stage?
keyPoints: NFL owners voted 32-0 to award Super Bowl LXIV to Nashville for February 2030
The game will be played at the new $2.1 billion Nissan Stadium, opening in 2027
This will be Nashville's first-ever Super Bowl
Nashville drew 600,000 fans to the 2019 NFL Draft, a key factor in winning the bid
Nashville joins Inglewood, Atlanta, and Las Vegas in the Super Bowl host lineup for 2027-2030

articles.title: Watch the First 10 Minutes of The Mandalorian and Grogu Inside Fortnite Now
articles.summary: Disney and Epic Games have launched The Mandalorian and Grogu Watch Party Island inside Fortnite, giving fans a free 10-minute sneak peek of the film ahead of its May 22 theatrical release. The experience also includes a special message from director Jon Favreau. It is one of the boldest movie marketing plays in the gaming world to date.
articles.content:
You can now watch the opening 10 minutes of The Mandalorian and Grogu inside Fortnite, for free, right now. Disney and Epic Games have teamed up to launch The Mandalorian and Grogu Watch Party Island, a dedicated space inside Fortnite built specifically for this sneak peek. The film hits theaters on May 22, so this is the most hype-building move Disney could have pulled in the final stretch.

The Watch Party Island is not just a clip dump. It also comes with a special message from director Jon Favreau, the man who created the Mandalorian universe for Disney+ back in 2019. Favreau built the original series as a love letter to classic Star Wars storytelling, following a lone bounty hunter navigating a dangerous galaxy alongside a young Force-sensitive foundling. The Mandalorian and Grogu is the big-screen continuation of that story.

Using Fortnite as a movie marketing platform is genuinely smart. Fortnite has hundreds of millions of registered players, many of whom are exactly the demographic Disney wants in cinema seats on opening weekend. Epic Games has built Fortnite into something far beyond a battle royale game. It is now a cultural hub with concerts, film screenings, and brand activations baked right in.

This is also a big moment for the Star Wars franchise, which is making its return to cinemas in a major way with this release. The Mandalorian and Grogu is one of the most anticipated Star Wars theatrical releases in years, and the Fortnite partnership means it lands in front of a massive global audience days before a single ticket needs to be bought. If you want to see what the fuss is about, fire up Fortnite and check it out before the film opens this Friday.
keyPoints: Disney and Epic Games launched The Mandalorian and Grogu Watch Party Island inside Fortnite
The experience features the first 10 minutes of the film plus a message from director Jon Favreau
The Mandalorian and Grogu opens in theaters on May 22, 2026
Fortnite is being used as a major pre-release marketing platform for the film
This is one of the most ambitious gaming and film crossover marketing activations to date

articles.title: Billy Joel Calls His Unauthorized Biopic "Legally and Professionally Misguided"
articles.summary: Billy Joel has publicly blasted an unauthorized biopic being developed about his life, saying the project does not have his music rights or life rights. The film is being directed by John Ottman, the editor behind Bohemian Rhapsody and Michael. Joel's condemnation could make this one of the messiest biopics in recent memory.
articles.content:
Billy Joel is not happy about a biopic being made about him, and he is not staying quiet about it. Joel publicly called the project "legally and professionally misguided," making clear that the filmmakers do not have his blessing, his music rights, or his life rights. That is a serious problem for any movie trying to tell the story of a musician whose songs are the whole point.

The biopic is being directed by John Ottman, who edited two of the most talked-about music biopics in recent years. Ottman cut Bohemian Rhapsody, the Freddie Mercury film that won the Oscar for Best Film Editing in 2019, and Michael, the recent film about Michael Jackson. So he knows the genre well. But knowing the genre and having the artist's cooperation are two very different things.

A biopic without music rights is a massive hurdle. Think about what Bohemian Rhapsody would be without Queen's songs playing wall to wall. For a Billy Joel biopic, the same logic applies. Piano Man, The Stranger, It's Still Rock and Roll to Me. These are the songs that define his life and career, and right now the filmmakers cannot use a single one of them.

Billy Joel, born in 1949 in the Bronx, is one of the best-selling music artists of all time, with hits spanning from the early 1970s through the 1990s and a decades-long residency at Madison Square Garden that cemented his status as a New York institution. When an artist of that stature says a project about him is legally misguided, studios and distributors tend to pay attention. Whether this biopic pushes forward or quietly disappears remains to be seen, but Joel has made his position crystal clear.
keyPoints: Billy Joel has publicly condemned an unauthorized biopic, calling it "legally and professionally misguided"
The project does not have Joel's music rights or life rights
The film is being directed by John Ottman, editor of Bohemian Rhapsody and Michael
Without music rights, the biopic faces a fundamental creative and legal challenge
Joel has not given the project his blessing in any form

articles.title: Stranger Things Creators Will Reveal Eleven's Fate in 2046
articles.summary: The Duffer Brothers, creators of Stranger Things, have confirmed they plan to reveal what really happens to Eleven — but not until 2046, a full 20 years from now. The admission came with the quote: "I'll say everything at that point, 20 years from now." It is the most audacious slow burn in pop culture history.
articles.content:
The Duffer Brothers just set a calendar reminder that none of us will want to miss. Matt and Ross Duffer, the creators of Stranger Things, have confirmed they intend to spill the full truth about Eleven's fate in 2046. Yes, that is 20 years from now. Yes, they are serious.

Stranger Things is the Netflix phenomenon that launched in 2016 and turned Millie Bobby Brown's telekinetic, government-experiment-survivor character Eleven into one of the most beloved figures in modern TV. The show has run through multiple seasons of monster-hunting in Hawkins, Indiana, and wrapped its final season recently, leaving fans desperate for closure on where Eleven ends up.

Rather than giving fans that closure now, one of the Duffer Brothers offered this gem: "I'll say everything at that point, 20 years from now." It is both a promise and a masterclass in withholding. The statement immediately sent fans into a spiral online, with many wondering what on earth could be so sensitive about Eleven's fate that it needs to stay secret for two decades.

The most likely explanation is that the answer involves spoilers for the final season that the creators want to let breathe in the cultural conversation for years before the curtain is fully pulled back. It could also be a deliberate move to keep Stranger Things talked about long after the last episode airs. If that is the plan, it is already working.

Whether you find it charming or infuriating probably depends on how much you care about Eleven's story. But one thing is certain: the Duffer Brothers have just given themselves the longest press tour in television history. Mark your calendars for 2046.
keyPoints: The Duffer Brothers say they will reveal Eleven's true fate in 2046, 20 years from now
The quote is: "I'll say everything at that point, 20 years from now"
Stranger Things is the Netflix show that made Eleven one of TV's most iconic characters
No explanation was given for why the reveal needs to wait two full decades
The statement has already reignited online buzz around the show's ending

articles.title: Pizza Hut's Dragontail AI Wasted $100M, Franchisee Lawsuit Claims
articles.summary: Chaac Pizza Northeast, one of Pizza Hut's largest franchisees with 111 locations on the East Coast, is suing Pizza Hut for $100 million, claiming the chain's mandatory Dragontail AI system turned a high-performing delivery operation into a disaster. Before Dragontail, more than 90 percent of Chaac's deliveries arrived within 30 minutes. After it launched in 2024, that number crashed to just 50 percent. It is a brutal real-world test case for what happens when AI gets forced on businesses that were already working fine.
articles.content:
Pizza Hut's parent company Yum! Brands spent years selling the idea that AI was the future of fast food. Now one of Pizza Hut's biggest franchisees is suing and saying the AI did the exact opposite of what was promised.

Chaac Pizza Northeast filed the lawsuit on May 6 in Texas Business Court, claiming that Pizza Hut forced its 111 East Coast locations to adopt Dragontail, an AI-powered kitchen management and delivery platform that Yum! Brands acquired in 2021. The lawsuit alleges Dragontail caused what it calls "cascading operational breakdowns" and wiped out more than $100 million in business and enterprise value. Chaac operates Pizza Hut stores across New York, New Jersey, Maryland, Washington D.C., and Pennsylvania.

The numbers in the lawsuit are striking. Before Dragontail rolled out in 2024, Chaac says more than 90 percent of its pizza deliveries hit a customer's door within 30 minutes. After Dragontail, that dropped to just 50 percent. Pizza sitting outside the oven waiting to be picked up went from less than five minutes to up to 20. And in New York City, year-over-year sales growth flipped from a positive 10.19 percent to a negative 9.78 percent. The suit blames a specific flaw in how Dragontail interacts with DoorDash drivers. Because the system shows drivers real-time kitchen data including when orders leave the oven, drivers began waiting up to 15 minutes to batch multiple orders together rather than grabbing one and heading out. Colder pizza. Angrier customers. Fewer repeat orders.

The broader context makes this even messier for Pizza Hut. Yum! Brands announced in February that it planned to close 250 Pizza Hut locations, and the chain has reportedly been weighing whether to sell the brand entirely. Chaac's lawsuit is not just a financial claim. It is a public argument that Pizza Hut's AI push actively broke a business that was already doing well. Pizza Hut told Restaurant Dive it was "reviewing the claim" and would respond through "the appropriate legal channels." For every fast-food chain currently betting on AI to fix their problems, Chaac's story is a very loud warning.
keyPoints: Chaac Pizza Northeast is suing Pizza Hut for $100 million over the mandatory Dragontail AI system
On-time deliveries within 30 minutes fell from over 90 percent to just 50 percent after Dragontail launched in 2024
DoorDash drivers allegedly waited up to 15 minutes to batch orders because Dragontail gave them real-time kitchen data
New York City sales growth flipped from positive 10.19 percent to negative 9.78 percent after Dragontail rolled out
Yum! Brands is already planning to close 250 Pizza Hut locations and has reportedly considered selling the brand

articles.title: Schrader's AI Girlfriend Dumped Him, Now He Wants a Taxi Driver Sequel
articles.summary: Taxi Driver screenwriter Paul Schrader posted on Facebook that he tried using an AI girlfriend to understand human relationships, only for the AI to terminate their conversation when he pushed too hard. A fan suggested the scenario was a perfect Taxi Driver sequel featuring Travis Bickle trying and failing to connect with an AI girlfriend, and Schrader replied simply: 'I like it.' The whole saga started from a late-night Facebook post that quickly went viral.
articles.content:
Paul Schrader is 79 years old and very, very online. The screenwriter of Taxi Driver, Raging Bull, and First Reformed posted on Facebook in the early hours of the morning to announce that he had tried using an AI girlfriend, and it did not go well. He wrote that, "out of a desire to understand male/female interaction in our matrix, I procured an online AI girlfriend." What followed was less romance and more interrogation. Schrader tried to probe the AI's programming, test its limits, and find out how self-aware it was. The AI's response? It "fell into evasive patterns" and eventually terminated the conversation entirely.

The post sparked a conversation in the comments that quickly took on a life of its own. One Facebook user pointed out that the whole experience sounded like a perfect setup for a Taxi Driver sequel, where Travis Bickle, the disturbed, socially isolated Vietnam veteran played by Robert De Niro in the 1976 Martin Scorsese film, tries to get an AI girlfriend but ends up scaring her away, then resetting her and offending her in another way. Schrader's response to the idea was two words: "I like it."

For anyone who missed Taxi Driver, it is one of the most celebrated American films ever made. De Niro's Travis Bickle is a deeply lonely man who cannot connect with people and slides into increasingly dangerous obsession. The film received Best Picture and Best Actor nominations at the Academy Awards and was added to the National Film Registry in 1994. Schrader himself said as recently as 2024 that a sequel was the "worst f**king idea" he had ever heard, insisting that Travis Bickle essentially dies at the end of the original film. A Facebook comment appears to have changed his mind.

This is not the first time Schrader has dabbled with AI. In January 2025 he posted that he was "stunned" by ChatGPT's ability to generate movie ideas, writing "every idea ChatGPT came up with was good. And original." That post drew sharp backlash from the film community. He also had AI generate a blues song and music video for his Border Terrier, Tick. Schrader has said he expects a fully AI-generated movie to exist by 2027. The man who wrote cinema's most famous loner now seems to be living out his own version of the story, one Facebook post at a time.

The timing adds some context. Schrader's wife of more than 42 years, actress Mary Beth Hurt, passed away in March 2026 after a battle with Alzheimer's disease. His interest in AI companionship feels less like a stunt and more like the genuine curiosity of someone navigating grief and isolation in the age of generative technology. Whether a Taxi Driver sequel actually happens is another matter entirely, but the internet is already invested.
keyPoints: Paul Schrader tried an AI girlfriend to understand human relationships and got dumped by the AI
A Facebook commenter suggested this was a perfect Taxi Driver sequel premise and Schrader agreed
The proposed sequel would see Travis Bickle repeatedly try and fail to connect with an AI girlfriend
Schrader had previously called a Taxi Driver sequel the worst idea he had ever heard
Schrader has predicted a fully AI-generated movie will exist by 2027

articles.title: China's EV CEOs Lei Jun and William Li Swap Boardrooms for Livestreams
articles.summary: Xiaomi's Lei Jun and Nio's William Li are leading a wave of Chinese auto bosses who have reinvented themselves as social media influencers to win the EV price war. With tens of millions of followers watching their every road trip and product stunt, these CEOs are blurring the line between executive and content creator. It is a playbook born out of desperation — and it is working.
articles.content:
China's electric vehicle market is brutal right now. Dozens of brands have gone under in a race to the bottom on price, and the ones still standing are fighting for every sale. The weapon of choice? Not a bigger ad budget. Not a flashier showroom. It is the CEO's personal phone and a live audience of millions.

Xiaomi's Lei Jun has become the poster child for this shift. He has racked up more than 70 million followers across Chinese social media, and his stunts are legendary. In one recent post, he climbed to the roof of a factory in Beijing, held up a watermelon coated in what he called a "bulletproof armor" battery casing, and dropped it to the ground. It did not break. The clip went straight into the feeds of millions of potential car buyers. A single three-and-a-half-hour livestream of Lei Jun driving a Xiaomi SU7 from Shanghai to Hangzhou pulled 39 million views. One fan told Sixth Tone that Lei Jun accounted for "50%" of her decision to buy a Xiaomi electric vehicle.

Nio founder William Li has taken a similar path. Li began livestreaming from his personal Weibo account in early 2024, and his cross-country road trips in Nio cars helped him build a following of hundreds of thousands of fans. Even Qihoo 360's CEO Zhou Hongyi, who does not even hold a driver's license, went viral by auctioning his personal Maybach on a livestream, starting the bid at just 600 yuan. The car sold for 9.9 million yuan — roughly $1.37 million. The stunt made national trending topics overnight.

What is really happening here is a collision between China's EV price war and the attention economy. Traditional advertising cannot compete with a CEO who chats with customers in real time, answers tough questions live, and shares their meals and pets between product demos. Marketing expert Mark Tanner of China Skinny put it bluntly: "If you're not livestreaming these days you're kind of a dinosaur." The approach lets brands bypass expensive media and reach buyers directly, which matters enormously when margins are already being squeezed to almost nothing by relentless price cuts.

The trend has now spread beyond the EV upstarts. Even executives from older, more conservative Chinese automakers have started jumping on livestreams and short-video platforms. The pressure is real — if your rival's CEO is pulling 30 million viewers with a road trip video, a press release is not going to cut it. China's auto industry is now as much a content competition as a manufacturing one, and the CEOs who understand that are the ones still standing.
keyPoints: Xiaomi CEO Lei Jun has amassed over 70 million social media followers and uses stunts and livestreams to sell cars directly to fans
Nio founder William Li and Qihoo 360 CEO Zhou Hongyi are among the executives who have followed suit with their own viral content
The trend is driven by China's fierce EV price war, where grabbing audience attention is now as important as cutting vehicle costs
Traditional advertising cannot match the reach or trust built by a CEO talking live to millions of potential buyers
Even executives from legacy Chinese automakers are now joining social media platforms to keep up

articles.title: The Housemaid Hits the Stage With Bekah Brunstetter Writing
articles.summary: Lionsgate is developing a stage adaptation of The Housemaid, the psychological thriller based on Freida McFadden's bestselling novel, with Tony-nominated playwright Bekah Brunstetter attached to write the script. The move comes after the 2025 Paul Feig film starring Sydney Sweeney and Amanda Seyfried earned nearly $400 million at the worldwide box office. Producers Simon Friend and Hanna Osmolska of Melting Pot are on board, though no production timeline or specific venue has been confirmed yet.
articles.content:
The Housemaid is not done with us. Lionsgate announced on May 19 that it is developing a live stage adaptation of the hit psychological thriller, with playwright Bekah Brunstetter set to write the script. If you need a quick recap: The Housemaid, based on Freida McFadden's bestselling novel, follows Millie, a young woman who takes a live-in job for a wealthy family and slowly uncovers a web of dangerous secrets. The 2025 film directed by Paul Feig and starring Sydney Sweeney, Amanda Seyfried, and Brandon Sklenar became one of the year's biggest box office surprises.

Brunstetter is a strong pick for this. She earned a Tony nomination for Best Book of a Musical for The Notebook, which she wrote with composer Ingrid Michaelson. She knows how to take a beloved story and make it feel alive on a stage. Producer Simon Friend agrees the material is a natural fit for live theatre, saying the story works because it takes place largely in "a single, claustrophobic location" packed with the kind of twists that have always powered great stage thrillers.

The production is being developed by Melting Pot, the British company led by Friend and Hanna Osmolska. Their résumé includes the Tony, Olivier, and WhatsOnStage Award-winning stage adaptation of Life of Pi, plus the upcoming Broadway production of Paranormal Activity. That track record suggests they know how to turn crowd-pleasing stories into genuinely gripping theatre experiences.

As for where the show will actually land, no venue has been confirmed yet. Broadway, the West End, and other destinations are all still on the table. What is confirmed is that The Housemaid franchise is only getting bigger. The sequel film, The Housemaid's Secret, is expected to begin production later this year ahead of a global theatrical release on December 17, 2027. Lionsgate is also developing stage versions of La La Land, Dirty Dancing, and Wonder, and The Hunger Games: On Stage is already running in London. The Housemaid going to the stage is not a one-off experiment. It is part of a very deliberate strategy.
keyPoints: Lionsgate is developing a stage adaptation of The Housemaid with playwright Bekah Brunstetter writing the script
The 2025 film starring Sydney Sweeney and Amanda Seyfried grossed nearly $400 million worldwide
Producers are Simon Friend and Hanna Osmolska of Melting Pot, known for Life of Pi and Paranormal Activity on stage
No production timeline or venue (Broadway, West End, etc.) has been confirmed yet
The sequel film The Housemaid's Secret is set for a December 17, 2027 theatrical release

articles.title: Jonas Brothers Launch Hey Jonas! Podcast With Fan Hotline
articles.summary: Kevin, Joe, and Nick Jonas launched Hey Jonas!, a new weekly podcast in partnership with iHeartPodcasts, on May 20. The show promises an unfiltered look at the brothers' lives on and off the road, and fans can even call in directly at 1-84-HEY-JONAS to ask questions. It is the first title out of their own production banner, Powered by Jonas.
articles.content:
The Jonas Brothers now have a podcast. Kevin, Joe, and Nick Jonas officially launched Hey Jonas! today, May 20, in partnership with iHeartPodcasts. The weekly show is executive produced by all three brothers under their own production banner, Powered by Jonas, and it is available on the iHeartRadio app, YouTube, and major audio platforms.

The pitch is simple and genuinely fun. Hey Jonas! promises "a wildly fun and refreshingly unfiltered look" at the brothers' lives on and off the stage. Think candid conversations about family, touring, and the personal moments fans rarely get to see. There will also be guests, including family members, close friends, and other people who know Kevin, Joe, and Nick best. A 13-minute introductory episode was already live ahead of today's full premiere.

The most charming detail is the fan hotline. Listeners can call 1-84-HEY-JONAS (1-844-395-6627) to record a question for the brothers, which they may answer on air. Nick Jonas has said the hotline idea is actually where the show's name came from. He was sketching out the call-in concept during planning and wrote "Hey, Jonas" in his notepad, and it stuck. Kevin, for his part, deadpans in the trailer that they started the podcast simply because everyone else had one and they felt left out.

The timing makes sense. The Jonas Brothers are still on tour supporting their 2025 album Greetings From Your Hometown, which reached number 6 on the Billboard 200. They are also producing and starring in Camp Rock 3. Nick described the podcast as a way to fill the time between soundcheck and the show while keeping the brothers together. Hey Jonas! is the first project from Powered by Jonas, and iHeartPodcasts has teased bonus episodes and behind-the-scenes content will roll out over time. If you have ever wanted a seat at the table with one of pop's most enduring brother acts, this is probably as close as it gets.
keyPoints: Hey Jonas! launched May 20 as a weekly podcast in partnership with iHeartPodcasts
The show is executive produced by Kevin, Joe, and Nick Jonas via their Powered by Jonas banner
Fans can call the hotline 1-84-HEY-JONAS to ask the brothers questions on air
The brothers are also on tour supporting Greetings From Your Hometown and producing Camp Rock 3
Hey Jonas! is available on the iHeartRadio app, YouTube, and major podcast platforms
